extracted_json.title: Managing Risk and Fast-Cycle Companies (TIM 172A/P)

items.text: In addition, the tech. company needs to be able to successfully manage RISK:

blocks.text: Companies that can successfully manage RISK, and at the same time develop high-quality products, rapidly, and at low cost are called FAST-CYCLE companies
Examples: Toyota, Intel, Microsoft, Google, Apple, Nvidia, Cisco, Seagate.
a few years ago : F A A N G
and now : M A N G A

The objective of this course, TIM 172A/P, is to develop the building blocks (tools, techniques,...) & a framework (or methodology) integrating these BBs, in order to be a fast-cycle company or competitor.

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Risk management and fast-cycle companies as the objective of course TIM 172A/P
extracted_json.summary_gist: Tech companies must manage technology (product development) and commercialization (market) risk. Companies that manage risk while developing high-quality products rapidly and at low cost are called fast-cycle companies (e.g., Toyota, Intel, Microsoft, Google, Apple, Nvidia, Cisco, Seagate; FAANG previously, MANGA now). The course TIM 172A/P aims to develop the building blocks and a framework to become a fast-cycle company.

transcription:
2. In addition, the tech. company needs to
be able to successfully manage RISK:
   (a) Technology (Product Development) RISK
   (b) Commercialization (Market) RISK

3. Companies that can successfully manage RISK,
and at the same time develop high-quality
products, rapidly, and at low cost are called
FAST-CYCLE companies

   Examples: Toyota, Intel, Microsoft,
             Google, Apple, Nvidia, Cisco,
             Seagate.

   a few years ago : F A A N G
                     [figure]
   Facebook, Amazon, Apple, Netflix,
                                Google

   and now        : M A N G A
                    [figure]
   Meta Apple Nvidia        same as
                            above

4. The objective of this course, TIM 172A/P,
is to develop the building blocks (tools, techniques,...)
& a framework (or methodology) integrating
these BBs, in order to be a fast-cycle
company or competitor.
